title: sim: Bring $print trigger/sampling semantics in line with FFs
body:
While trying to add more tests to get better coverage for the code I added for #4128 (which had quite a few bugs not uncovered by our current tests) I noticed that `sim`'s sampling behavior of enable and arguments for `$print` with a trigger does not match how we treat edge triggered FFs. In particular that makes it impossible to use `sim` to test that `clk2fflogic` doesn't change the semantics of a design that should be supported by `clk2fflogic`.

I think it could also lead to quite confusing behavior when for Verilog inputs, inside an edge triggered process there is an assignment to a register next to a print of the same expression that is assigned to the register. ~~I haven't yet tried to reproduce that exact scenario (I've focused on the more complex `clk2fflogic` testcases) but I think the previous behavior could show a mismatch of what is printed and what assignment is made (or even whether an assignment is made) while the new behavior should match.~~ In simple cases and basically for everything you would intentionally write as part of a design, it makes no difference though, you actually need to have an enable or input signal changing simultaneously with the trigger signal, which you would want to generally avoid as it's a race condition. FV still needs to provide consistent behavior across all engines and simulations within the same flow, though, and for code written as part of a formal test bench there might even be valid uses of this.

I briefly looked at what cxxrtl does, but with #2746 (and maybe related issues) AFAICT it currently doesn't support the testcases that I need to exercise clk2fflogic. ~~I do plan to perform some simpler tests to make sure that sim and cxxrtl agree on what is printed for those.~~ All test cases where this change makes a difference would run into #2746 and I think even with that fixed cxxrtl might consider those to be race conditions and not want to specify the exact behavior. 

@povik in any case I wanted to get some early feedback from you, in case I'm missing something that prompted the current design and would break with my changes

I'm not sure about the `trg_on_gclk` attribute, but while testing `clk2fflogic` I realized I need some way to have a `$print` without trigger produce output at most once every gclk cycle while also outputting every gclk cycle it is enabled, even when no enable or argument changed (essentially to emulate a one full update per fixed cycle simulation).

The alternative would be to agree on a way to explicitly represent that by some parametrization of the `$print` cell, but given that the exact behavior of a trigger-less side effect is inherently coupled to the simulation strategy (e.g. also when there are glitches) and the fact that `sim` isn't a fixed cycle simulation but it tries to emulate one for the FV flows, I think using an attribute to switch between two behaviors that I'd consider to be in the scope of the allowed implementation defined behaviors for a trigger-less `$print` cell is not the worst solution.
